title: Tickboard UI: roadmap view (epic chains, status colors, gate badges)
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 (tickboard is the priority roadmap surface). Consumes GET /api/roadmap shipped by the server tick (merged before this runs): waves of epics with id, title, status (done|active|ready|queued|gated), awaiting_type, blocked_by, children_total, children_closed.

Recon facts (verified): internal/tickboard/ui/src — Lit + Nanostores + Shoelace; entry main.ts registers elements; root ticks-app.ts; main board components/tick-board.ts (~2000 lines, kanban with mobile breakpoints); stores in src/stores (comms.ts fetch wrappers); Vitest via pnpm test (run pnpm install + pnpm test in internal/tickboard/ui); Catppuccin Mocha variables in src/styles/catppuccin.css.

Implement:
1. types + fetch: add RoadmapResponse types and fetchRoadmap() following existing api/stores conventions (api/ticks.ts, stores/comms.ts); a $roadmap nanostore that refetches on the existing SSE tick-update events.
2. components/roadmap-view.ts: new Lit component rendering waves vertically — each epic as a card/row: status color (done=green --green, active=blue --blue, ready=yellow --yellow with needs-planning label, queued=surface/gray, gated=peach --peach with awaiting badge), [closed/total] progress, blocked-by ids as small chips. Vertical list layout that works on mobile (no SVG connectors needed — wave grouping + chips carry the structure). Use ticks-*/Shoelace components and Catppuccin variables per CLAUDE.md; custom events bubbles:true composed:true.
3. Integration: register in main.ts; add a board/roadmap toggle where tick-board.ts hosts view switches (follow however tickflow-dashboard.ts is toggled); clicking an epic card opens the existing tick-detail-drawer for that epic.
4. Tests (Vitest): roadmap-view renders waves/statuses/badges from a fixture RoadmapResponse; store refetch logic. Run pnpm test in internal/tickboard/ui plus go build ./... (UI embeds may require the build script — check scripts/build-ui.sh; run it if the Go embed expects fresh assets, and say so in your report).

Per project CLAUDE.md: pnpm only; never confirm()/alert(); Geist fonts already global.

Run: (cd internal/tickboard/ui && pnpm install && pnpm test) && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: pnpm test passes incl. new roadmap-view tests; toggle shows the roadmap with correct status colors/badges; epic click opens detail drawer
